claims.claim: Urban single-ride fare is 8 NIS, raised from 6 NIS on April 25, 2025.
claims.raw_snippet: as of the reform implementation date (Friday, April 25, 2025), the fare for a single bus trip will also increase by NIS 2

claims.claim: A ~12% hike to ~9 NIS was announced for June 2026 but is contested and may be deferred.
claims.raw_snippet: 'It will not happen': Regev opposes potential public transport price hikes; fares expected to rise by up to 12% in June, with single bus fare from 8 NIS to approximately 9 NIS

claims.claim: Nationwide monthly pass (excluding Israel Railways, up to 225km) is approximately 315 NIS.
claims.raw_snippet: nationwide pass costs 315 NIS for unlimited rides on all types of public transport except Israel Railways, up to a distance of 225 km, for a full month

claims.claim: Ministry of Transport (PTI) approves 5 payment apps: Rav-Pass (HopOn), Moovit, Pango, Cellopark, egg.
claims.raw_snippet: Moovit, רב-פס (Rav-Pass), פנגו (Pango), סלופארק (Cello Park), egg

claims.claim: Jerusalem Light Rail Green Line first segment (Malha to Binyanei Hauma) opens May 2026; full line scheduled 2027.
claims.raw_snippet: Green Line is scheduled to open in 2027; first phase Malha to HaTurim opening May 2026

claims.claim: Jerusalem Light Rail Blue Line construction began September 2025, gradual opening 2029-2031.
claims.raw_snippet: Construction on the Blue Line began in September 2025; expected to gradually open between 2029 and 2031

claims.claim: Tel Aviv Light Rail Red Line operational since August 2023, 34 stations.
claims.raw_snippet: Red Line operational since August 18, 2023; 34 stations across Tel Aviv, Petah Tikva, Bnei Brak, Ramat Gan, and Bat Yam

claims.claim: Tel Aviv Red Line daily ridership is around 110-120k in 2026, below the original 238k forecast.
claims.raw_snippet: daily average is still fluctuating around 110-120,000 passengers per day, far from the original forecasts of 238,000

claims.claim: Tel Aviv Light Rail Purple Line expected to open end of 2027 to 2028; 27km, 43 stations.
claims.raw_snippet: Purple Line: Under construction. Expected to open around 2027. 27-kilometer line with 43 stations

claims.claim: Tel Aviv Light Rail Green Line southern segment 2028, northern terminus Herzliya 2030; 39km / 62 stops. Egged won operator tender.
claims.raw_snippet: Egged wins tender to operate TA light rail Purple, Green Lines; Green Line southern segment Holon-Rishon LeZion now scheduled to open in 2028, full opening Herzliya planned for 2030; 39 kilometers, 62 stops

claims.claim: Israel Railways launched a new schedule on January 17, 2026 adding 40k+ daily seats and bi-level trains on Emek and Carmel lines.
claims.raw_snippet: במוצאי שבת, 17 בינואר 2026, הושק לוח זמנים חדש; תוספת של מעל 40 אלף מקומות ישיבה ביום, רכבות דו-קומתיות בקו העמק ובקו הכרמל

claims.claim: Eastern Railway (Hadera-Lod, 65km, 4 new stations) partial opening 2026, full 2026-2027.
claims.raw_snippet: 65 kilometers from Lod to Hadera, doubling tracks over 50 kilometers, four passenger stations; partial operations expected by 2025 and full opening in 2026

claims.claim: Tel Aviv-Jerusalem fast line takes ~32 min via Yitzhak Navon station, every 30 min.
claims.raw_snippet: Opened in 2018, the Jerusalem-Tel Aviv high-speed line reducing journey time between Israel's two largest cities to just 32 minutes; trains every half hour

claims.claim: Ministry of Transport GTFS portal at gtfs.mot.gov.il has shown intermittent outages in 2026.
claims.raw_snippet: The requested content is not available, please contact the system administrator (Hebrew error)

claims.claim: Iron Swords war has caused emergency operations including pauses on specific rail corridors.
claims.raw_snippet: Israel has shifted its public transit networks to emergency operations; pauses in rail services such as the Coastal Line pause on February 13, 2026

claims.claim: Jerusalem Light Rail Red Line: 35 stops, 22.5 km.
